About Cirata
Cirata, accelerates data-driven revenue growth by automating data transfer and integration to modern cloud analytics and AI platforms without downtime or disruption. With Cirata, data leaders can leverage the power of AI and analytics across their entire enterprise data estate to freely choose analytics technologies, avoid vendor, platform, or cloud lock-in while making AI and analytics faster, cheaper, and more flexible. Cirata's portfolio of products and technology solutions make strategic adoption of modern data analytics efficient and automated. For more information about Cirata, visit www.cirata.com.

latest.title: Amendment to Notice of Annual General Meeting
latest.content:
Today 08:52
RNS Number : 6608B
Cirata PLC
23 April 2026
23 April 2026
Cirata plc
("Cirata" or the "Company")
Amendment to Notice of Annual General Meeting
The Company announces that, following publication of the Notice of Annual General Meeting (the "Notice") on 31 March 2026, it has subsequently added a resolution relating to the adoption of a
Restricted Share Unit plan
(the
"
RSU Plan
"), which the Board intends to include in business of the Annual General Meeting (the "AGM").
Accordingly, the Company will shortly post to shareholders an
amended Notice of
AGM
(the
"
Amended Notice
"), which includes an additional resolution to approve the adoption of the RSU Plan. The Amended Notice will also be available on the Company's website
here
.
The RSU Plan is intended to support the Company's ongoing efforts to attract, retain and incentivise key employees and management, aligning their interests with those of shareholders as the Company continues to execute its strategy.
AGM details
As previously announced, the AGM will be held on
19 May 2026
at
11 am
, at the offices of Brown Rudnick LLP, 8 Clifford Street, London W1S 2LQ.
Action to be taken by shareholders
Shareholders should note the following:
·
The Amended Notice, together with a revised Form of Proxy, is being sent directly to shareholders
·
Shareholders who have already submitted a Form of Proxy are encouraged to review the Amended Notice and, if they wish to vote on the additional resolution or amend their existing voting instructions, should submit a new Form of Proxy.
·
The submission of a new Form of Proxy will supersede any prior proxy submitted.
·
Shareholders who do not wish to change their previously submitted proxy instructions are not required to take any action.
General
Save for the inclusion of the additional resolution described above, all other resolutions set out in the original Notice remain unchanged.
For further information, please contact:
Cirata

Cirata plc
("Cirata" or the "Company")
Grant of Awards & PDMR Dealing
Cirata (LSE: CRTA) announces that on 20 April 2026, a grant of awards was made to Stephen Kelly (CEO), Paul Scott Murphy (CTO) and Dominic Arcari (CRO) under the Company's Share Options Plan (as amended and restated) (together, the "Awards").
The Company's Remuneration Committee has approved the Awards in relation to the Company wide FY25 bonus and regards long term incentives as an important part of the remuneration of members of the leadership team to align them with shareholder interests. The Awards are set out in further detail below.
The exercise price of the Awards is 16.9p (equal to the 60-day volume weighted average price to 14 April 2026) and carry no performance conditions.

company_30s.what_they_do: Cirata delivers secure, cloud‑native data integration and analytics services that let organisations move, clean and analyse data across multiple silos. The platform is built for regulated sectors such as insurance, finance and healthcare, where data sovereignty and compliance are critical.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Data is the new oil, but most firms struggle to stitch together disparate data sources while meeting privacy rules. Cirata’s platform solves that pain point by providing a single, compliant layer that can ingest, transform and expose data to downstream applications and AI tools. Its contracts with large insurers and data‑centric clients demonstrate that the market needs this capability.
company_30s.current_state: Cirata is in a turnaround phase: it has just returned to the market after a block listing, has refreshed its management team and announced a series of multi‑year contracts. The company is still burning cash but is showing signs of stabilisation with new revenue streams and a clearer go‑to‑market strategy. The share price sits on the lower end of the company’s valuation range, reflecting the market’s wariness about the company’s cash position and recent governance changes.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy on the regulatory front: AGM results, a trading statement, and a block listing return suggest that the board is tightening governance and trying to rebuild investor confidence.

Business‑side news shows a steady stream of contract wins – a multi‑year data integration deal worth £700k, a £3.1m multi‑year agreement with a US insurer, and a £6.7m contract win earlier this year – signalling that the platform is gaining traction in key markets.

Operational moves such as the divestment of its DevOps division and the launch of a new data platform indicate that Cirata is trimming non‑core assets and focusing on its core data‑integration proposition.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism amid a cautious crowd
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish voices focus on the new contracts and the fact that Cirata is finally addressing data sovereignty – a hot regulatory trend. They point out that the company’s platform is positioned for growth in regulated industries.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish chatter highlights the block listing, the recent management shake‑up, and the company’s cash burn. Some investors fear that the new contracts may not be enough to offset the high operating costs and that the company could still be in a precarious financial position.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Earlier this month sentiment was almost silent; as contract announcements piled up, a faint but growing thread of optimism emerged. The narrative now leans toward a potential rebound, but many still remain wary of the cash runway.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is flat – no public search activity. That means the market has largely ignored the story so far, leaving room for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: Upcoming earnings release – a better than expected revenue or a clear cash‑flow improvement could trigger a bounce.
New contract signings announced at the next AGM or through a press release – any fresh multi‑year deal would validate the platform’s traction.
A spike in trading volume during the earnings call or a breakout above the recent swing high would signal that traders are starting to take notice.
A positive regulatory update (e.g., a new data‑safety certification or a favorable FCA ruling) could lift confidence in the company’s compliance posture.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2–3 months include: a sustained uptick in revenue, a reduction in the cash burn rate, the signing of a large contract, and any change in the company’s debt structure (e.g., a refinancing or a new equity raise). Technical confirmation would be a clean break above the last swing high with volume exceeding the 20‑day average.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share price appears to have completed a deep capitulation – it has fallen well below its 52‑week low and the APEX score indicates a tight squeeze. The next move will likely be a mean‑reversion bounce once the market sees a credible catalyst. The stock is still in the early phase of that bounce, so patience is required before committing capital.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The company is sitting on a deep crash (over 70 % drop) and the latest contract wins demonstrate that the platform is gaining real market traction. If the share price recovers to the middle of its 52‑week range, the upside is roughly 200 % from the current level, while the downside is capped at the depth of the crash. The historical performance of deep‑crash signals shows that when a catalyst arrives, the rebound can be explosive.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the cash burn continues to outpace the new revenue, forcing another capital raise or a downgrade. The block listing and recent management changes suggest that investor confidence is fragile. If earnings fail to improve or a large contract falls through, the stock could remain stuck at the bottom.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 70 % of the invested capital if the price stays at the current low. Potential gain: 200 % if the price climbs to the mid‑range. The asymmetry (2 : 1 upside to downside) favours a contrarian stance.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a confirmed earnings beat or a new contract announcement that lifts the price above the last swing high. Size the position to 5–10 % of your discretionary equity. Place a stop just below the recent low to protect against a further crash. Use a trailing stop to lock in profits as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a clear volume spike accompanying a breakout above the recent high, or until the company announces a new multi‑year contract. Only then consider a small position to test the waters.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified; the only red flag would be a sudden delisting or a bankruptcy filing. In the absence of such events, the setup remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Cirata’s deep crash, combined with fresh contracts and a silent market, sets the stage for a potentially explosive mean‑reversion – a 2‑to‑1 upside with a 70 % downside cap.
